value.source_note: This king is only attested as filiation in the name of the next king, his son Kay; see Ryholt 1997a, 219.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt ḫꜥ m wꜣst sꜥnḫ tꜣwy
value.anglicised: ka nakht kha em waset, sankh tawy
value.translation: The victorious bull who has appeared in Thebes and sustained the Two Lands
Victorious bull, beloved of Re
attested_in: Barque Stand, Avaris
value.source_note: KRI I, 232–34; the quartzite stand, erected in the area of Khatâna-Qantir, is dedicated to the god Seth. The names are found on the right side of the upper register of text, from a scene showing the king tying the symbols of Upper and Lower Egypt together; see Habachi 1974, 97, fig. 2.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt dr ḫꜣswt m nḫtw.f
value.anglicised: ka nakht der khasut em nakhtu.ef
value.translation: The victorious bull who has repelled foreign lands with his victories
attested_in: Obelisk, Heliopolis (main text, south face)
value.source_note: KRI I, 118–20; PM VII, 409 (2). The east face of the obelisk was inscribed for Ramses II.

value.translation: Victorious bull, beloved of Re and Maat

value.translation: The victorious bull who has appeared in the White Crown, the beloved of Re
attested_in: Temple, Kanais
value.source_note: KRI I, 70–71; Kanais is in the Wadi Mia, Eastern Desert, east of Edfu. As is often the case, the king's original titulary is found on the temple's main texts; the additional names given here were carved on the pillars in the main hall; see KRI I, 71:14 and the plan in PM VII, 322.

See Daressy 1920; Sourouzian 1989, no. 16, pp. 60–61, pl. 11a; and Iskander 2002, 161, no. 3.5.3.

value.transliteration: kꜣ pḥty sḫm-ḫpš nḫt-ꜥ nb nrw m tꜣw ḫꜣswt fnḫw tmḥw
value.anglicised: ka pehty, sekhem khepesh, nakht-a, neb neru em tau khasut fenkhu temehu
value.translation: Powerful bull, potent of sword, strong-armed, lord of dread in the lowlands and the highlands of the Fenkhu-Asiatics and the Temehu-Libyans
attested_in: Medinet Habu, southern end of the First Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 2). KRI V, 49:14.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt mꜣi pḥty nḫt-ꜥ nb ḫpš ḥꜣḳ sttiw
value.anglicised: ka nakht mai pehty, nakht a, neb khepesh, haq setetiu
value.translation: Victorious bull and powerful lion, strong-armed, the lord of strength who has captured the Asiatics
attested_in: Medinet Habu, western end of the First Court, exterior of the Second Pylon
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 3). KRI V, 37:10.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt mnḫ-mnw sḥtp nb-r-ḏr m nꜣy.f ꜣḫwt
value.anglicised: ka nakht menekh menu, sehetep neb-er-djer em nay.ef akhut
value.translation: Victorious bull, splendid of monuments, who has satisfied the Lord of All with his benefactions
attested_in: Medinet Habu, eastern end of the Second Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 4). A designation of the god Re (Lord of All). KRI V, 314:8.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt swsḫ kmt wsr-ḫpš nḫt-ꜥ smꜣ ṯḥnw
value.anglicised: ka nakht sewesekh kemet, weser khepesh, nakht a, sema tjehenu
value.translation: The victorious bull who has widened (the boundaries of) Egypt, the one powerful of sword and strong of arm who has slaughtered the Libyans
attested_in: Medinet Habu, northern end of the Second Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 5). KRI V, 20:14.

value.transliteration: kꜣ nḫt-ꜥ dm ḥnwty mn-ib wr-pḥty ḥr bꜣwy n ḳnw
value.anglicised: ka nakht-a, dem henuty, men ib, wer pehty her bawy en qenu
value.translation: The strong-armed bull who has sharpened (his) two horns, decisive and great of strength on the battlefield of bravery
attested_in: Medinet Habu, northern end of the Second Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 6). Lit. "strong-minded." KRI V, 315:11.

value.translation: With many monuments
attested_in: Medinet Habu, eastern end of the Second Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 7). Lit. "the one of many monuments." KRI V, 316:6.

value.transliteration: sḫm-pḥty hd ḥfnw dḫ nꜣ pḥw sw dmḏ (ẖr) ṯbwy.f(y)
value.anglicised: sekhem pehty, hed hefnu, dekh na pehu su, demedj (kher) tjebwy.fy
value.translation: Powerful of strength, who has attacked hundreds of thousands, overthrown those who have attacked him, and gathered (them) (under) his sandals
attested_in: Medinet Habu, exterior north wall of the Second Court
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 8). KRI V, 30:14.

value.transliteration: ꜥn ḥr srḫ mi sꜣ ꜣst
value.anglicised: an her serekh mi sa aset
value.translation: Beautiful on the throne like the son of Isis
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 9). Hölscher 1941, pl. 32 (a).

value.transliteration: bity wr-mnw wr-biꜣwt mḥ ipt-swt ḥr rn.f
value.anglicised: bity, wer menu, wer biaut, meh ipet-sut her ren.ef
value.translation: The king of Lower Egypt, great of monuments and great of wonders, who has filled Ipet-sut with his renown
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 10). Or perhaps simply "The reigning king," as suggested in the introduction; see Quirke 1986, 123–24. Lit. "name." Hölscher 1941, pl. 32 (a).

value.translation: Lord of a lifetime like his father Re
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 11). Hölscher 1941, pl. 32 (a).

value.translation: Son of Amun
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 12). Hölscher 1941, pl. 32 (a).

value.transliteration: sꜥꜣ ḫꜥw mi ꜣḫty wbn.f ꜥnḫ rḫyt
value.anglicised: saa khau mi akhty weben.ef, ankh rekhyet
value.translation: Who has magnified (his) appearances like the One of the Two Horizons when he rises, the (very) life of the rekhyet-people
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 13). Lit. "made great." Hölscher 1941, pl. 32 (a).

value.translation: Possessor of Sed festivals like Ta-tjenen
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 14). Hölscher 1941, pl. 35 (b).

value.translation: Dazzling of appearances
attested_in: Medinet Habu, various monuments found outside the Royal Palace
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 15). Hölscher 1941, pl. 35 (b).

value.transliteration: ꜥn n nsw mi itmw mr.tw.f ꜣb.tw.f mi ḥm n rꜥ
value.anglicised: an en nesu mi itemu, mer.tu.ef ab.tu.ef mi hem en ra
value.translation: Beautiful as king like Atum, he is loved and wished for like the majesty of Re
attested_in: Karnak, Ramses III's temple in the precinct of Mut
value.source_note: Additional Horus name (Horus 16). KRI V, 289:3.

value.translation: The Ruler of Foreign Lands Semqen ("He is my gift")
value.source_note: Leprohon labels as 'Title and name' — the Hyksos `ḥqꜣ ḫꜣswt` ('Ruler of Foreign Lands') ruler-title is attested fused with the king's personal name; dual-emitted to both `throne_names` and `birth_names` for downstream matching. Von Beckerath 1999, 116–17. For the rendering, see T. Schneider 1998, 137–38. The attribution of this king to the Fifteenth Dynasty is uncertain; see Ryholt 1997a, 383.

value.anglicised: hunu nefer, bener-merut, tjeni su nebty rekhyet hena ka.f, dua en.ef khnum, shepes er shesep en.ef kha em nesu, sensen.en sehnu em haaw mi nedjet it.ef tjehen mesu(t) her neset it.ef mi hor ka nakht, ity pesedj em ta-mery mi hapu ankh, redi en.ef hebu-sed ashau weru mi ptah ta-tjenen it netjeru
value.translation: The perfect youth sweet of love, whom the Two Mistresses and the common folk have elevated along with his ka, whom the august Khnum praised in order to assume the crown of kingship for himself, with whom commanders have readily (lit. "in joy") associated like the Protector-of-his-Father, dazzling of birth on the throne of his father like Horus, the victorious bull, the sovereign who shines in Ta-mery like the living Apis bull, to whom has been given a great many Sed festivals like Ptah Ta-tjenen, the father of the gods
value.source_note: Gauthier 1916, 392–410; von Beckerath 1999, 244–45. 'Protector-of-his-Father' = Horus.

leprohon-33.13|horus_names.rationale: 3-arbiter blind re-extraction (chunk=macedonian-ptolemaic): 2/3 arbiters converged. Per-arbiter matches_agent: ['c']. Basis (arbiter d): Leprohon p. 186 (Ptolemy XII Neos Dionysos Auletes) Original-titulary Horus name. Footnote 51 = scholarly chain (Gauthier 1916, 392–410; von Beckerath 1999, 244–45); footnote 52 = 'Horus' glossing 'Protector-of-his-Father' in the translation. Agent c keeps the translation faithful to Leprohon's main

value.transliteration: iwꜥ n pꜣ nṯr nty nḥm stp n ptḥ ir mꜣꜥt rꜥ sḫm ꜥnḫ imn
value.anglicised: iwa en pa netjer nety nehem, setep en ptah, ir maat ra, sekhem ankh imen
value.translation: Heir of the god-who-rescues, chosen by Ptah, who has accomplished the Maat of Re, the living image of Amun

value.transliteration: iwꜥ n pꜣ nṯr nty nḥm stp n ptḥ ir mꜣꜥt imn-rꜥ
value.anglicised: iwa en pa netjer nety nehem, setep en ptah, ir maat imen-ra
value.translation: Heir of the god-who-rescues, chosen by Ptah, who has accomplished the Maat of Amun-Re
attested_in: From an architrave at the temple of Philae; see Gauthier 1916, 401, XXXII

value.transliteration: pꜣ nṯr mry it sn wsir ḥwnw
value.anglicised: pa netjer, mery it sen, wesir hunu
value.translation: The god who is beloved of his father and brother, the youthful Osiris ('Neos Dionysos')
attested_in: From a reused block found near the temple of Hathor at Philae; see Gauthier 1916, 401, XXXV

leprohon-33.13|throne_names.rationale: 3-arbiter blind re-extraction (chunk=macedonian-ptolemaic): full-JSON vote was 1/1/1 but 2/3 arbiters matches_agent='b'. Differences across the 3 arbiters are in non-IDENTIFIER framing (typically source_note phrasing); resolving to agent b's original value as the closest unmodified fit.

value.translation: The great one, possessor of perfection and splendid of shrine
value.source_note: Gauthier 1916, 410–22; von Beckerath 1999, 244–45. Chauveau (1997, 62) points out that, like her ancestor Cleopatra I, Cleopatra VII was not given a full titulary, possibly out of reluctance to accept the fact of having a queen on the throne.

value.translation: The great one and the (very) image of her father
attested_in: Part of a scene from the temple of Armant, showing scenes of the divine birth of her son Caesarion; see Gauthier 1916, 417–17, XVIII,H

leprohon-33.14|horus_names.rationale: 3-arbiter blind re-extraction (chunk=macedonian-ptolemaic): 2/3 arbiters converged. Per-arbiter matches_agent: ['b', 'c']. Basis (arbiter d): Leprohon pp. 187–188 (Cleopatra VII Philopator). Variant 2 transliteration: source line 634 prints 'wrt twt n it.s' — no parentheses on wrt. Agents b and c match the source verbatim; agent a fabricates parentheses '(wr(t))' to mirror variant 1's feminine markers (constitutional rule: do not invent).
